_provenance.proposal_block: idea-engine control/outbox.md @ main 636e21d6f4158eef83664f92a9ad584938177fbd (landed via idea-engine PR #444)
_provenance.verdict: VERDICT 092 (P079 -> V092, the +13 offset's sixteenth row)
_provenance.lane_pin: menno420/superbot-idle @ 884aeae9687742a389a2e2086a4cc930e5a4f3ee (the drafter's registered pin, re-fetched FIRSTHAND this session on a fresh public-HTTPS clone; the lane's HEAD had not moved)
_provenance.firsthand: Every committed constant below was re-verified on the fresh clone BEFORE this fixture was written: the ladder constants and fold, the runtime grant and _prestige re-grant, the dispatch verb set, the lock-line composition, the seven committed sentences, the 18-pack catalog censuses (roster/base-rate multisets, zero balance blocks), and the theme gate + schema bounds. The vendored tree below is byte-identical to the clone (sha256 per file).
_provenance.committed: 2026-07-16, BEFORE the runner (the P017-P078 hermetic pre-registered discipline)
_provenance.rational_convention: all decision arithmetic is exact integer arithmetic on the vendored engine; no rationals, no floats in any decision arm (the V090 value-pair rule is moot on this head - every registered numeral is a plain integer)

vendor.layout: vendor/ mirrors the lane's repo layout (idle_engine/, tools/, schema/, themes/, docs/design/) so tools/play.py and tools/theme_gate.py resolve REPO_ROOT to vendor/ unchanged
vendor.third_party_note: the vendored engine's theme loader imports PyYAML and the vendored theme gate imports jsonschema - the lane's own committed dependencies (theme_gate.py docstring: 'the theme-gate workflow pip-installs it'); the sim's decision computations are stdlib + the vendored engine only

ladder.fold: base_rate * count * upgrade_pct * prestige_pct * milestone_pct * theme_pct // 100_000_000 (one floor per generator per second, idle_engine/engine.py)
ladder.grant: new_session: owned = {gid: start_count for gid in theme.generators} if start_count else {}; _prestige re-grants the identical set after apply_prestige (tools/play.py)

canonical_corpus.note: REGISTERED BY SHAPE (11 commands, full dispatch surface incl. a full prestige cycle via offline 200000); this exact listing is the sim-chosen realization, disclosed as a vacancy-derived fixture per the registration's own convention controls (F5: a corpus extension must not flip R1). 'prestige do' is eligible on every s>=1 cell after the 200000 s offline span and REFUSED on every s=0 cell (the registered F5 degeneracy control).

lock_lines.note: the two registered byte-forms; owned-2/3 render the same frozen metric against 100 / 1,000 via the engine's _format_amount

dial_cells.dial_cell_convention: pre/post rates around the first award boundary; the (3000, 3450) cell is evaluated on the registered owned-only pinned GameStates (earned sets {}, {owned-1}, {owned-1..3}) because a live 1 s span at 3000/s crosses lifetime-1 and would pollute the owned-only reading - disclosed, per the registration's own wedge-probe-state pin

lag_pairs.world: two-gen pack, s=5, spans measured on consecutive advance() calls; the 3600 s second span runs at pct 115 (owned-1 + lifetime-1 + lifetime-2 banked together at the first boundary)

sentence_pins.normalization: whitespace-collapse (every run of whitespace -> one space) then substring containment in the named vendored file

pins.text: The starting grant is a RUNTIME entrypoint choice (see the module docstring) — it lives entirely in this tool and touches no economy constant.
the engine has no generator purchase verb yet (a separate, economy-number-bearing slice), so without a starting grant a fresh save produces nothing and there is no loop to watch.
No pay-to-win: no purchase path exists or is planned that buys milestones or their bonuses.
rung 1 is a first-session target once generator purchase lands
no non-neutral value ships without Q-0264 approval
no generator purchase path exists yet in the engine

armR.role: REPORTING-ONLY (no statistical gate; the registered previews are reproduction anchors)
armR.draw_order_grammar: one random.Random(seed) per trace set; per trace, draws in exactly this order: (1) pack index uniform over the sorted 18-id list [randrange(18)], (2) flag s uniform over the registered grid tuple [randrange(12) indexing the tuple], (3) L uniform 3..10 [randint(3,10)], then per step exactly TWO draws: (4a) command index uniform over the 5-tuple ('wait','offline','buy-max','prestige-do','achievements') [randrange(5)], (4b) duration uniform 60..7200 [randint(60,7200)], drawn EVERY step and consumed only by wait/offline
armR.preview_triple: (invariance violations across all trace boundaries, blessed traces, boundaries)

disclosed_conventions.boundaries: sum of L over the 2,000 traces (one boundary per dispatched command)
disclosed_conventions.blessed: a trace whose live earned set contains at least one owned rung at trace end; closed form (verified equal, a self-check): config s*n >= theta_1 AND the trace contains at least one wait/offline step (the only award-carrying boundaries reachable from a fresh save - buy needs funds that only wait/offline produce, 'prestige do' needs lifetime >= 100,000, 'achievements' renders without awarding)
disclosed_conventions.buy_max_mapping: 'buy-max' dispatches 'buy <first-declared-upgrade-id> max' (boost1 on every shipped pack)
disclosed_conventions.vacancy_note: the registered previews pin the TRIPLES; these two byte-level conventions are sim-chosen, disclosed here, and ledgered as vacancy-derived disclosures - the registered triples are the match claims

witness_packs.construction: invented-but-pinned (the registration's own class: a 10-generator base-20 pack and its 9-generator twin, base_rate 20 = the smallest committed-bounds rate where +5% pays exactly at count 1, ledger-clean 20*105//100 = 21). Ids/nouns are sim-chosen skin; the packs differ ONLY in roster length (asserted field-by-field, F5).
witness_packs.gate_run: the REAL vendored tools/theme_gate.py as a subprocess over an assembled 20-pack catalog directory (the 18 shipped packs + both witnesses); recorded facts are path-free (exit code, pack count line)

typed_contacts.C1: 18 == vendored themes/*.yaml glob count == census rows
typed_contacts.C2: 54 == 18*3 == frozen rendered owned fields at the default flag (byte-stable across the whole corpus)
typed_contacts.C3: the four margin-0 rows: s**n == theta exactly AND earned at s* AND refused at s*-1
typed_contacts.C4: Arm-A closed-form predicate {owned-k : s*n >= theta_k} == Arm-B live award set on all 648 cell-rungs (first boundary AND post-corpus)

decision_rule.REJECT: R1 AND R2 AND R3 AND R4 (the owned track, as shipped, is a configuration predicate - achievements-v0's progression framing and play.py's touches-no-economy-constant sentence cannot both stand)
decision_rule.R1: invariance (total owned == s*n at every boundary of the canonical corpus on all 216 cells) AND dichotomy (earned set == closed-form predicate at the first boundary and after the full corpus, 648 cell-rungs) AND the prestige re-grant fixed point
decision_rule.R2: catalog census: exactly (18 packs, roster multiset {1x1, 2x17}, 54 skinned owned slots) AND at the default flag all 54 unreachable with lock lines exactly '🔒 2 / 10' / '🔒 1 / 10', byte-stable across the corpus
decision_rule.R3: flip thresholds exactly ceil(theta/n) with the four margin-0 rows earned-at-s*/not-at-s*-1 AND the wedge table exact (10->10, 19->19, 20->21; 30->31 with the 5->5 / 25->26 split; 3000->3450) AND the boundary-lag pairs (900/930, 108000/118800)
decision_rule.R4: the constructed 10/9-generator pair passes the committed theme gate (exit 0, 20 packs) AND the 10-gen pack is born-blessed at the default flag with rates exactly 210 vs 180
decision_rule.APPROVE: some committed no-load command sequence at the default flag changes total owned or earns any owned rung (the V017-unlock-verb witness world satisfies it; mutually exclusive with REJECT by construction)
NULL_axes: the load escape hatch (load is blob-authoritative by committed design; persistence validates types, not rosters - every reachability claim is scoped to the no-load command surface)
the corpus/grid convention (R1 is also structural: single-writer + fixed point; corpus-extension and grid-bump must not flip it - carried as F5 controls)
the future-mechanic boundary (the ruling binds the SHIPPED surface at 884aeae only; V017's conditional registration row is the named arming event)
decision_rule.evaluator_inputs: ENUMERATED: {R1, R2, R3, R4, approve_witness} booleans only - the twin evaluators receive no other values

wedge_states.probe: owned-only earned sets {}, {owned-1}, {owned-1,owned-2,owned-3} on pinned GameStates (the registration's invented-but-pinned wedge probe states)

gates.F1: identities: 18 = pack glob = census rows; 54 = 18*3; roster multiset {1x1,2x17}; base-rate multiset {1x18,5x17}; 216 = 18*12; 648 = 216*3; the seven sentence pins present (whitespace-normalized) at the vendored pin
gates.F2: theorems T1-T6 re-derived from scratch on the vendored engine (zero trust in the idea file's numbers)
gates.F4: pencil worlds: 30*30 = 900; 31*30 = 930; the prestige fixed point {tier1: 5, tier2: 5}; 3450 = 3000 + 15% exactly with no floor loss at that fold (575 + 2875)
gates.F5: degeneracy/convention controls: s=0 (empty grant, zero production, prestige refused, invariant 0); corpus-extension and grid-bump non-flipping on R1; the back-door pair differs ONLY in roster length (field-by-field)
gates.F6: battery: twin evaluators exact-equal through the four typed contacts; Arm-R determinism (each seed reproduces itself byte-identically) and the draw-order grammar asserted by draw-count sentinels (3 + 2L draws per trace)
